change_reason: FAB-14 WI-4426: amend DCL-ARTIFACT-APPROVAL-HOOK-001 to document formal and narrative packet auto-discovery per DELIB-FAB14-REMEDIATION-20260610; bridge gtkb-fab-14-gate-fp-feedback-loop GO at -008.
explicit_change_request: AUQ DELIB-FAB14-REMEDIATION-20260610: Owner selected HYG-047: add deterministic packet auto-discovery to both narrative and formal approval gates.
full_content:
Any hook, skill, CLI flow, or harness integration that converts user input into formal GT-KB artifacts must render the complete proposed artifact before canonical persistence. The renderer must not summarize away required metadata or omit content that will be stored.

The approval event must capture proposed artifact ID and type, source conversation or source artifact, full proposed content hash, approval mode (approve / acknowledge / edit-and-approve / auto), and the explicit change request statement. The hook must also persist a transcript record of the display-and-acknowledge moment for downstream audit.

The constraint applies to the following implementation surfaces and path patterns:

1. Bash-mediated KB mutations (existing): the formal-artifact-approval-gate.py PreToolUse hook intercepts Bash commands matching formal-mutation patterns (gt spec record/update, gt deliberations add/upsert/link, insert_spec(...), update_spec(...), etc.) and requires a packet via GTKB_FORMAL_APPROVAL_PACKET env var or --formal-approval-packet=<path> flag. As of FAB-14 / WI-4426, when no explicit packet reference is supplied, the hook may deterministically auto-discover the newest packet under .groundtruth/formal-artifact-approvals/ whose artifact_id matches the command --id or --artifact-id value and whose full_content_sha256 matches the sha256 of the command --content-file contents. Missing, mismatched, malformed, or invalid packets remain blocking.

2. Narrative-artifact file writes (added by GTKB-NARRATIVE-ARTIFACT-APPROVAL-EXTENSION-001 Slice A.1, commit 68364ea8): the narrative-artifact-approval-gate.py PreToolUse hook intercepts Write/Edit on paths matching the protected pattern set in config/governance/narrative-artifact-approval.toml (.claude/rules/*.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, CLAUDE-REFERENCE.md, CLAUDE-ARCHITECTURE.md, memory/work_list.md) and requires a packet referencing the staged blob's sha256. As of FAB-14 / WI-4426, when no env var or explicit tool_input packet hint is supplied for a Write call carrying full content, the hook may deterministically auto-discover the newest packet under .groundtruth/formal-artifact-approvals/ whose target_path equals the protected write path and whose full_content_sha256 matches the proposed content. Content-less Edit calls and mismatched packets still block unless an explicit valid packet is supplied.

3. Universal-floor pre-commit gate (added by GTKB-NARRATIVE-ARTIFACT-APPROVAL-EXTENSION-001 Slice C, commit d85c20ce): scripts/check_narrative_artifact_evidence.py is invoked from .githooks/pre-commit and validates each staged narrative-artifact path against an approval packet under .groundtruth/formal-artifact-approvals/. This gate runs at git-commit time regardless of which AI harness produced the staged change -- it is the load-bearing harness-agnostic enforcement floor per ADR-CODEX-HOOK-PARITY-FALLBACK-001.

Path-pattern exemptions are enumerated in config/governance/narrative-artifact-approval.toml [[exemptions]] blocks (currently: memory/pending-owner-decisions.md hook-managed; .claude/rules/*.local.md local-override convention).

Future implementation surfaces (e.g., Slice B AUQ decision-class transport once spike concludes) extend this constraint additively without removing existing surfaces.
